name: molmass
documentation:
# Molecular mass calculations

Molmass is a Python library, console script, and web application to calculate
the molecular mass (average, nominal, and isotopic pure), the elemental
composition, and the mass distribution spectrum of a molecule given by its
chemical formula, relative element weights, or sequence.

Calculations are based on the isotopic composition of the elements. Mass
deficiency due to chemical bonding is not taken into account.

The library includes a database of physicochemical and descriptive properties
of the chemical elements.

```
Author

Christoph Gohlke

License

BSD 3-Clause

Version

2024.10.25

DOI

10.5281/zenodo.7135495
```

## Quickstart

Install the molmass package and all dependencies from the
[Python Package Index](https://pypi.org/project/molmass/):

```
python -m pip install -U "molmass[all]"
```

Print the console script usage:

```
python -m molmass --help
```

Run the web application:

```
python -m molmass --web
```

The molmass library is documented via docstrings.

See [Examples] for using the programming interface.

Source code and support are available on
[GitHub](https://github.com/cgohlke/molmass).

## Requirements

This revision was tested with the following requirements and dependencies
(other versions may work):

- [CPython](https://www.python.org) 3.10.11, 3.11.9, 3.12.7, 3.13.0
- [Flask](https://pypi.org/project/Flask/) 3.0.3 (optional)
- [Pandas](https://pypi.org/project/pandas/) 2.2.3 (optional)
- [wxPython](https://pypi.org/project/wxPython/) 4.2.2 (optional)

## Revisions

2024.10.25

- Fix composition of formula with multiple isotopes of same element (#16).

2024.5.24

- Fix docstring examples not correctly rendered on GitHub.

2024.5.10

- Add options to disable parsing groups, oligos, fractions, arithmetic (#14).
- Add Formula.expanded property.

2023.8.30

- Fix linting issues.
- Add py.typed marker.
- Remove support for Python 3.8.

2023.4.10

- Support rdkit-style ionic charges (#11, #12).
- Enable multiplication without addition in from_string.

2022.12.9

- Fix split_charge formula with trailing \]\] (#11).

2022.10.18

- Several breaking changes.
- Add experimental support for ion charges (#5).
- Change Element, Isotope, and Particle to dataclass (breaking).
- Change types of Spectrum and Composition (breaking).
- Add functions to export Spectrum and Composition as Pandas DataFrames.
- Replace lazyattr with functools.cached_property.
- Rename molmass_web to web (breaking).
- Change output of web application (breaking).
- Run web application using Flask if installed.
- Add options to specify URL of web application and not opening web browser.
- Convert to Google style docstrings.
- Add type hints.
- Remove support for Python 3.7.

2021.6.18

- Add Particle types to elements (#5).
- Fix molmass_web failure on WSL2 (#9).
- Fix elements_gui layout issue.
- Remove support for Python 3.6.

2020.6.10

- Fix elements_gui symbol size on WSL2.
- Support wxPython 4.1.

2020.1.1

- Update elements atomic weights and isotopic compositions from NIST.
- Move element descriptions into separate module.
- Remove support for Python 2.7 and 3.5.

2018.8.15

- Move modules into molmass package.

2018.5.29

- Add option to start web interface from console.
- Separate styles from content and use CSS flex layout in molmass_web.

2018.5.25

- Style and docstring fixes.
- Make from_fractions output deterministic.
- Accept Flask request.args in molmass_web.
- Style and template changes in molmass_web.

2016.2.25

- Fix some elements ionization energies.

2005.x.x

- Initial release.

## Examples

Calculate the molecular mass, elemental composition, and mass distribution of
a molecule from its chemical formula:

```pycon
>>> from molmass import Formula
>>> f = Formula('C8H10N4O2')  # Caffeine
>>> f
Formula('C8H10N4O2')
>>> f.formula  # hill notation
'C8H10N4O2'
>>> f.empirical
'C4H5N2O'
>>> f.mass  # average mass
194.1909...
>>> f.nominal_mass  # == f.isotope.massnumber
194
>>> f.monoisotopic_mass  # == f.isotope.mass
194.0803...
>>> f.atoms
24
>>> f.charge
0
>>> f.composition().dataframe()
         Count  Relative mass  Fraction
Element...
C            8      96.085920  0.494801
H           10      10.079410  0.051905
N            4      56.026812  0.288514
O            2      31.998810  0.164780
>>> f.spectrum(min_intensity=0.01).dataframe()
             Relative mass  Fraction  Intensity %         m/z
Mass number...
194             194.080376  0.898828   100.000000  194.080376
195             195.082873  0.092625    10.305100  195.082873
196             196.084968  0.008022     0.892492  196.084968
197             197.087214  0.000500     0.055681  197.087214
```

Access physicochemical and descriptive properties of the chemical elements:

```pycon
>>> from molmass import ELEMENTS, Element
>>> e = ELEMENTS['C']
>>> e
Element(
    6, 'C', 'Carbon',
    group=14, period=2, block='p', series=1,
    mass=12.01074, eleneg=2.55, eleaffin=1.262118,
    covrad=0.77, atmrad=0.91, vdwrad=1.7,
    tboil=5100.0, tmelt=3825.0, density=3.51,
    eleconfig='[He] 2s2 2p2',
    oxistates='4*, 2, -4*',
    ionenergy=(
        11.2603, 24.383, 47.877, 64.492, 392.077,
        489.981,
    ),
    isotopes={
        12: Isotope(12.0, 0.9893, 12),
        13: Isotope(13.00335483507, 0.0107, 13),
    },
)
>>> e.number
6
>>> e.symbol
'C'
>>> e.name
'Carbon'
>>> e.description
'Carbon is a member of group 14 of the periodic table...'
>>> e.eleconfig
'[He] 2s2 2p2'
>>> e.eleconfig_dict
{(1, 's'): 2, (2, 's'): 2, (2, 'p'): 2}
>>> str(ELEMENTS[6])
'Carbon'
>>> len(ELEMENTS)
109
>>> sum(e.mass for e in ELEMENTS)
14693.181589001...
>>> for e in ELEMENTS:
...     e.validate()
...     e = eval(repr(e))
...
```

modules.name: molmass.web
documentation:
Molecular Mass Calculator web application.

Run the web application in a local web server:

```
python -m molmass.web
```

The application is run in a Flask built-in server, and a web browser is opened.

If Flask is not installed, the application is run in a built-in CGI server.
The cgi module is deprecated and slated for removal in Python 3.13.
To run in CGI mode, this script must be made executable on UNIX systems:

```
chmod -x ./web.py
```

Do not run the built-in Flask or CGI servers in a production deployment.
Instead, for example, create a Flask app and serve it on a production server:

```
# app.py
from molmass.web import response
from flask import Flask, request
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/', methods=['GET'])
def root():
    return response(request.args, url=request.base_url)
```
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: molmass.web.response
documentation:
Return HTML document from submitted web form.

Parameters:

: form: Flask.request.form or cgi.FieldStorage.
  url: URL of web application.
  template: HTML page template. The default is {py:attr}`PAGE`.
  help: Help text. The default is {py:attr}`HELP`.
  heads: Additional HTML page head sections.

functions.name: molmass.web.analyze
documentation:
Return analysis of formula as HTML string.

Parameters:
    formula: Chemical formula.
    maxatoms: Number of atoms below which to calculate spectrum.
    min_intensity: Minimum intensity to include in spectrum.

functions.name: molmass.web.isotopes
documentation: Return HTML table of isotope masses and abundances.

functions.name: molmass.web.groups
documentation: Return HTML table of chemical groups.

functions.name: molmass.web.webbrowser
documentation:
Open url in web browser after delay.

Parameters:
    url: URL to open in web browser.
    delay: Delay in seconds before opening web browser.

functions.name: molmass.web.cgi
documentation:
Run web application in local CGI server.

Parameters:
    url: URL at which the web application is served.
    open_browser: Open `url` in web browser.
    debug: Enable debug mode.

functions.name: molmass.web.main
documentation:
Run web application in local Flask or fallback CGI server.

Parameters:
    form:
        Form data to use on first run.
    url:
        URL at which the web application is served.
        The default is 'http://127.0.0.1:5001/'.
    open_browser:
        Open `url` in web browser.
    debug:
        Enable debug mode.

modules.name: molmass.elements
documentation:
Properties of the chemical elements.

Chemical elements, particles, and isotopes are represented as Python class
instances. Physicochemical and descriptive properties of are accessible as
attributes.

Data sources:

1. https://www.nist.gov/pml/
   atomic-weights-and-isotopic-compositions-relative-atomic-masses
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/{element.name}

functions.name: molmass.elements.sqlite_script
documentation:
Return SQL script to create sqlite database of elements.

Examples:
    >>> import sqlite3
    >>> con = sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
    >>> cur = con.executescript(sqlite_script())
    >>> con.commit()
    >>> for r in cur.execute("SELECT name FROM element WHERE number=6"):
    ...     str(r[0])
    ...
    'Carbon'
    >>> con.close()

Relative atomic mass.

The ratio of the average mass of atoms of the element to 1/12 of the mass
of an atom of 12C.

documentation: Electronegativity on Pauling scale.

documentation: Covalent radius in Angstrom.

documentation: Atomic radius in Angstrom.

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.vdwrad

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.tboil

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.tmelt
documentation: Melting temperature in K.

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.density

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.eleconfig

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.oxistates

class_variables.name: molmass.elements.Element.ionenergy

Relative mass.

Particle, e.g., electron, proton, or neutron.

modules.name: molmass.molmass
documentation:
Molecular mass calculations.

Molmass is a Python library, console script, and web application to calculate
the molecular mass (average, nominal, and isotopic pure), the elemental
composition, and the mass distribution spectrum of a molecule given by its
chemical formula, relative element weights, or sequence.

Calculations are based on the isotopic composition of the elements. Mass
deficiency due to chemical bonding is not taken into account.

The library includes a database of physicochemical and descriptive properties
of the chemical elements.

```
Author

Christoph Gohlke

License

BSD 3-Clause

Version

2024.10.25

DOI

10.5281/zenodo.7135495
```

O            2      31.998810  0.164780

Mass number...
194             194.080376  0.898828   100.000000  194.080376
195             195.082873  0.092625    10.305100  195.082873

>>> e.number
6

'C'

'Carbon'

'Carbon is a member of group 14 of the periodic table...'

'[He] 2s2 2p2'

{(1, 's'): 2, (2, 's'): 2, (2, 'p'): 2}
>>> str(ELEMENTS[6])

functions.name: molmass.molmass.analyze
documentation:
Return analysis of chemical formula as string.

Parameters:
    formula: Chemical formula.
    maxatoms: Number of atoms below which to calculate spectrum.
    min_intensity: Minimum intensity to include in spectrum.
    debug: If True, raise exceptions, else print error message.

Examples:
    >>> print(analyze('C8H10N4O2', min_intensity=0.01))
    Formula: C8H10N4O2
    Empirical formula: C4H5N2O
    <BLANKLINE>
    Nominal mass: 194
    Average mass: 194.19095
    Monoisotopic mass: 194.08038 (89.883%)
    Most abundant mass: 194.08038 (89.883%)
    Mean of distribution: 194.18604
    <BLANKLINE>
    Number of atoms: 24
    <BLANKLINE>
    Elemental Composition
    <BLANKLINE>
    Element  Count  Relative mass  Fraction %
    C            8       96.08592     49.4801
    H           10       10.07941      5.1905
    N            4       56.02681     28.8514
    O            2       31.99881     16.4780
    <BLANKLINE>
    Mass Distribution
    <BLANKLINE>
    A    Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    194      194.08038   89.882781   100.000000
    195      195.08287    9.262511    10.305100
    196      196.08497    0.802196     0.892492
    197      197.08721    0.050048     0.055681

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_string
documentation:
Return formula string from user input string.

Parameters:
: formula:

  : Chemical formula.
    Supports simple, non-nested arithmetic (+ and \*), abbreviations
    of common chemical groups, peptides, oligos, and mass fractions.

  groups:

  : Mapping of chemical group name to formula in Hill notation.
    The default is {py:attr}`GROUPS`.

  parse_groups:

  : Parse chemical group names.

  parse_oligos:

  : Parse pure peptide and nucleotide sequences.

  parse_fractions:

  : Parse list of mass fractions.

  parse_arithmetic:

  : Parse simple arithmetic operators.

Returns:

: Chemical formula composed only of element and isotope symbols,
  parentheses, numbers, and trailing ion charges.

Raises:

: FormulaError: Invalid formula.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from_string('Valohp')
  '(C5H8NO2)'
  >>> from_string('Valohp', parse_groups=False)
  'Valohp'
  >>> from_string('HLeu2OH')
  'H(C6H11NO)2OH'
  >>> from_string('D2O')
  '[2H]2O'
  >>> from_string('O: 0.26, 30Si: 0.74')
  'O2[30Si]3'
  >>> from_string('O: 0.26, 30Si: 0.74', parse_fractions=False)
  'O:0(,30Si:0)26()74'
  >>> from_string('PhNH2.HCl')
  '(C6H5)NH2HCl'
  >>> from_string('PhNH2.HCl', parse_arithmetic=False)
  '(C6H5)NH2.HCl'
  >>> from_string('CuSO4.5H2O')
  'CuSO4(H2O)5'
  >>> from_string('CuSO4+5*H2O')
  'CuSO4(H2O)5'
  >>> from_string('5*H2O')
  '(H2O)5'
  >>> from_string('O2+12C')  # not [12C]O2
  'O2(C)12'
  >>> from_string('ssdna(AC)')
  '((C10H12N5O5P)(C9H12N3O6P)H2O)'
  >>> from_string('peptide(GG)')
  '((C2H3NO)2H2O)'
  >>> from_string('WQ')
  '((C5H8N2O2)(C11H10N2O)H2O)'
  >>> from_string('WQ', parse_oligos=False)
  'WQ'
  ```

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_elements
documentation:
Return formula string in Hill notation from elements dict.

Parameters:
: elements:

  : Number of atoms and isotopes by element.
    See {py:meth}`Formula._elements`.

  divisor:

  : Number by which to divide element counts and charge.

  charge:

  : Charge number.

  fmt:

  : Format strings.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from_elements({'C': {0: 4, 12: 2}}, divisor=2)
  'C2[12C]'
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> from_elements(
  ...     {'C': {0: 4, 12: 2}},
  ...     divisor=2,
  ...     fmt=(
  ...         '{}',
  ...         '{}<sub>{}</sub>',
  ...         '<sup>{}</sup>{}',
  ...         '<sup>{}</sup>{}<sub>{}</sub>',
  ...     ),
  ... )
  'C<sub>2</sub><sup>12</sup>C'
  ```

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_fractions
documentation:
Return formula string from elemental mass fractions.

Parameters:
    fractions: Mapping of element symbols to abundances.
    maxcount: Maximum count of single element in formula.
    precision: Threshold for finding smallest acceptable element count.

Examples:
    >>> from_fractions({'H': 0.112, 'O': 0.888})
    'H2O'
    >>> from_fractions({'D': 0.2, 'O': 0.8})
    'O[2H]2'
    >>> from_fractions({'H': 8.97, 'C': 59.39, 'O': 31.64})
    'C5H9O2'
    >>> from_fractions({'O': 0.26, '30Si': 0.74})
    'O2[30Si]3'

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_sequence
documentation:
Return sequence as chemical formula.

Parameters:
: sequence:

  : DNA, RNA, or peptide sequence.

  groups:

  : Mapping of sequence item to chemical formula in Hill notation.
    One of {py:attr}`DEOXYNUCLEOTIDES`, {py:attr}`NUCLEOTIDES`, or
    {py:attr}`AMINOACIDS`.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from_sequence('A', {'A': 'B'})
  '(B)'
  >>> from_sequence('AA', {'A': 'B'})
  '(B)2'
  ```

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_peptide
documentation:
Return chemical formula for polymer of unmodified amino acids.

Parameters:
    sequence: Perptide sequence.

Examples:
    >>> from_peptide('GG')
    '((C2H3NO)2H2O)'

    >>> f = Formula(from_peptide('GPAVL IMCFY WHKRQ NEDST_2+'))
    >>> print(f.formula, f.atoms, f.mass)
    [C107H159N29O30S2]2+ 327 2395.7168...

functions.name: molmass.molmass.from_oligo
documentation:
Return chemical formula for polymer of unmodified (deoxy)nucleotides.

Each strand includes a 5' monophosphate.

Parameters:
    sequence:
        DNA or RNA sequence.
    dtype:
        Nucleic acid sequence type.
        One of 'ssdna', 'dsdna', 'ssrna', or 'dsrna'.

Examples:
    >>> from_oligo('AC', 'ssdna')
    '((C10H12N5O5P)(C9H12N3O6P)H2O)'

    >>> from_oligo('AU', 'dsrna')
    '((C10H12N5O6P)2(C9H11N2O8P)2(H2O)2)'

    >>> f = Formula(from_oligo('ATC G', 'dsdna'))
    >>> print(f.formula, f.atoms, f.mass)
    C78H102N30O50P8 268 2507.60913...

    >>> f = Formula(from_oligo('AUC G_2+', 'ssrna'))
    >>> print(f.formula, f.atoms, f.mass)
    [C38H49N15O29P4]2+ 135 1303.7744...

functions.name: molmass.molmass.hill_sorted
documentation:
Return iterator over element symbols in order of Hill notation.

Parameters:
    symbols: Element symbols.

Yields:
    Element symbols in order of Hill notation.

Examples:
    >>> tuple(hill_sorted('HCO'))
    ('C', 'H', 'O')

functions.name: molmass.molmass.gcd
documentation:
Return greatest common divisor of integer numbers.

Using Euclid's algorithm.

Parameters:
    numbers: Integer numbers.

Examples:
    >>> gcd([4])
    4
    >>> gcd([3, 6])
    3
    >>> gcd([6, 7])
    1

functions.name: molmass.molmass.precision_digits
documentation:
Return number of digits after decimal point to print f in width chars.

Parameters:
    f: Floating point number to print in `width` characters.
    width: Maximum length of printed number.

Examples:
    >>> precision_digits(-0.12345678, 5)
    2
    >>> precision_digits(1.23456789, 5)
    3
    >>> precision_digits(12.3456789, 5)
    2
    >>> precision_digits(12345.6789, 5)
    1

functions.name: molmass.molmass.split_charge
documentation:
Return formula stripped from charge, and charge.

Parameters:
    formula: Chemical formula with appended charge.

Returns:
    Chemical formula without charge, and charge number.

Examples:
    >>> split_charge('Formula')
    ('Formula', 0)
    >>> split_charge('Formula+')
    ('Formula', 1)
    >>> split_charge('Formula+1')
    ('Formula', 1)
    >>> split_charge('Formula++')
    ('Formula', 2)
    >>> split_charge('[Formula]2+')
    ('Formula', 2)
    >>> split_charge('Formula+2')
    ('Formula', 2)
    >>> split_charge('[[Formula]]2-')
    ('[Formula]', -2)
    >>> split_charge('[Formula]-2')
    ('Formula', -2)
    >>> split_charge('[Formula]_2-')
    ('Formula', -2)
    >>> split_charge('Formula_2-')
    ('Formula', -2)
    >>> split_charge('Formula-2')
    ('Formula', -2)
    >>> split_charge('Formula_+')
    ('Formula', 1)
    >>> split_charge('Formula+-')
    ('Formula', 0)
    >>> split_charge('Formul+a+')
    ('Formul+a', 1)

functions.name: molmass.molmass.join_charge
documentation:
Return formula with charge appended.

Parameters:
    formula: Chemical formula without charge.
    charge: Charge number.
    separator: Character separating formula from charge. One of '' or '_'.

Returns:
    Formula with charge != 0 appended.
    If `separator` is empty, the formula is wrapped in square brackets.

Examples:
    >>> join_charge('Formula', 0)
    'Formula'
    >>> join_charge('Formula', 1)
    '[Formula]+'
    >>> join_charge('Formula', 2)
    '[Formula]2+'
    >>> join_charge('Formula', -2, '_')
    'Formula_2-'

functions.name: molmass.molmass.format_charge
documentation:
Return string representation of charge.

Parameters:

: charge: Charge number.
  prefix: Character placed in front of charge in case `abs(charge)>1`.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> format_charge(-2, '_')
  '_2-'
  >>> format_charge(1, '_')
  '+'
  ```

functions.name: molmass.molmass.test
documentation:
Test module.

Parameters:
    verbose: Print status of testing.

functions.name: molmass.molmass.main
documentation:
Command line usage main function.

Parameters:
    argv: Command line arguments.

classes.name: molmass.molmass.Formula

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula._elements
documentation:
Number of atoms and isotopes by element.

A `dict[symbol: dict[massnumber: count]]`, where `massnumber` is
either an isotope mass number or zero for a natural distribution
of isotopes.

Raises {py:class}`FormulaError` if formula is invalid.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H')._elements
{'H': {0: 1}}
>>> Formula('[2H]2O')._elements
{'O': {0: 1}, 'H': {2: 2}}
```

```
meta public

```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.formula
documentation:
Formula string in Hill notation.

```pycon
>>> Formula('BrC2H5').formula
'C2H5Br'
>>> Formula('[(CH3)3Si2]2NNa').formula
'C6H18NNaSi4'
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.empirical
documentation:
Empirical formula in Hill notation.

The empirical formula has the simplest whole number ratio of atoms
of each element present in the formula.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H2O').empirical
'H2O'
>>> Formula('C6H12O6').empirical
'CH2O'
>>> Formula('[SO4]2_4-').empirical
'[O4S]2-'
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.expanded
documentation:
Formula string with expanded groups, charges, and sequences.

The input formula parsed by {py:func}`from_string`.

```pycon
>>> Formula('WQ').expanded  # peptide sequence
'((C5H8N2O2)(C11H10N2O)H2O)'
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.atoms
documentation:
Number of atoms.

```pycon
>>> Formula('CH3COOH').atoms
8
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.charge
documentation:
Charge number in units of elementary charge.

```pycon
>>> Formula('SO4_2-').charge
-2
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.gcd
documentation:
Greatest common divisor of element counts.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H2').gcd
2
>>> Formula('H2O').gcd
1
>>> Formula('C6H12O6').gcd
6
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.mass
documentation:
Average relative molecular mass.

The sum of the relative atomic masses of all atoms and charges in
the formula.
Equals the molar mass in g/mol, that is, the mass of one mole of
substance.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H').mass
1.007941
>>> Formula('H+').mass
1.007392...
>>> Formula('SO4_2-').mass
96.06351...
>>> Formula('12C').mass
12.0
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').mass
194.1909...
>>> Formula('C48H32AgCuO12P2Ru4').mass
1438.404...
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.monoisotopic_mass
documentation:
Mass of isotope composed of most abundant elemental isotopes.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').monoisotopic_mass
194.08037...
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.nominal_mass
documentation:
Monoisotopic mass number.

The number of protons and neutrons in the isotope composed of the most
abundant elemental isotopes.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').nominal_mass
194
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.mz
documentation:
Mass-to-charge ratio.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H').mz
1.007941
>>> Formula('H+').mz
1.007392...
>>> Formula('SO4_2-').mz
48.03175...
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.isotope
documentation:
Isotope composed of most abundant elemental isotopes.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C').isotope.mass
12.0
>>> Formula('13C').isotope.massnumber
13
>>> Formula('C48H32AgCuO12P2Ru4').isotope
Isotope(mass=1439.588..., abundance=0.00205..., massnumber=1440...)
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.composition
documentation:
Return elemental composition.

Parameters:
    isotopic:
        List isotopes separately as opposed to part of an element.

Examples:
    >>> print(Formula('[12C][13C]C').composition())
    Element  Count  Relative mass  Fraction %
    C            1      12.010740     32.4491
    12C          1      12.000000     32.4201
    13C          1      13.003355     35.1308

    >>> print(Formula('[12C][13C]C').composition(False))
    Element  Count  Relative mass  Fraction %
    C            3      37.014095    100.0000

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.spectrum
documentation:
Return low resolution mass spectrum.

Calculated by combining the mass numbers of the elemental isotopes.

Parameters:
    min_fraction: Minimum of fraction to return.
    min_intensity: Minimum intensity to return.

Returns:
    Mapping of massnumber to mass, fraction, and intensity.

Examples:
    >>> print(Formula('D').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    2      2.0141018  100.000000   100.000000

    >>> print(Formula('H').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    1      1.0078250   99.988500   100.000000
    2      2.0141018    0.011500     0.011501

    >>> print(Formula('H+').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    1      1.0072765   99.988500   100.000000
    2      2.0135532    0.011500     0.011501

    >>> print(Formula('D2').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    4      4.0282036  100.000000   100.000000

    >>> print(Formula('DH').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    3      3.0219268   99.988500   100.000000
    4      4.0282036    0.011500     0.011501

    >>> print(Formula('H2').spectrum())
    A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    2      2.0156501   99.977001   100.000000
    3      3.0219268    0.022997     0.023003
    4      4.0282036    0.000001     0.000001

    >>> print(Formula('DHO').spectrum())
    A   Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
    19      19.016841   99.745528   100.000000
    20      20.021536    0.049468     0.049594
    21      21.021087    0.204981     0.205504
    22      22.027363    0.000024     0.000024

    >>> print(Formula('SO4_2-').spectrum(min_intensity=0.1))
    A   Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %  m/z
    96      95.952827   94.070057   100.000000  47.976413
    97      96.952996    0.886071     0.941927  48.476498
    98      97.949936    4.983307     5.297443  48.974968

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.__mul__
documentation:
Return formula repeated number times.

```pycon
>>> Formula('HO-') * 2
Formula('[(HO)2]2-')
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.__rmul__
documentation:
Return formula repeated number times.

```pycon
>>> 2 * Formula('H2O')
Formula('(H2O)2')
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.__add__
documentation:
Add this and other formula.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H2O') + Formula('HO-')
Formula('[(H2O)(HO)]-')
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Formula.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract elements of other formula.

```pycon
>>> Formula('H2O') - Formula('O')
Formula('H2')
```

Chemical formula.

Calculate various properties from formula string, such as hill notation,
empirical formula, mass, elemental composition, and mass distribution.

Parameters:
: formula:

  : Chemical formula. May contain only symbols of chemical elements,
    groups, isotopes, parentheses, numbers, and trailing charge.
    The formula is not validated until it is parsed on demand
    during instance attribute access or method calls.

  groups:

  : Mapping of chemical group name to formula in Hill notation.
    The default is {py:attr}`GROUPS`.

  parse_groups:

  : Parse chemical group names. Enabled by default.

  parse_oligos:

  : Parse pure peptide and nucleotide sequences. Enabled by default.

  parse_fractions:

  : Parse list of mass fractions. Enabled by default.

  parse_arithmetic:

  : Parse simple arithmetic operators. Enabled by default.

Examples:

: Elements and counts:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('H2O')
  Formula('H2O')
  ```

  Isotopes:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('D2O')
  Formula('[2H]2O')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('[30Si]3O2')
  Formula('[30Si]3O2')
  ```

  Ion charges:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('[AsO4]3-')
  Formula('[AsO4]3-')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('O2-2')
  Formula('[O2]2-')
  ```

  Abbreviations of chemical groups:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('EtOH')
  Formula('(C2H5)OH')
  ```

  Simple arithmetic:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('(COOH)2')
  Formula('(COOH)2')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('CuSO4.5H2O')
  Formula('CuSO4(H2O)5')
  ```

  Relative element weights:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('O: 0.26, 30Si: 0.74')
  Formula('O2[30Si]3')
  ```

  Nucleotide sequences:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('CGCGAATTCGCG')
  Formula('((C10H12N5O5P)2(C9H12N3O6P)4(C10H12N5O6P)4(C10H13N2O7P)2H2O)')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('dsrna(CCUU)')
  Formula('((C10H12N5O6P)2(C9H12N3O7P)2...(C9H11N2O8P)2(H2O)2)')
  ```

  Peptide sequences:

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('MDRGEQGLLK')
  Formula('((C4H5NO3)(C5H7NO3)(C2H3NO)2...(C6H12N4O)H2O)')
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> Formula('peptide(CPK)')
  Formula('((C3H5NOS)(C6H12N2O)(C5H7NO)H2O)')
  ```

classes.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem

methods.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem.astuple
documentation: Return (symbol, count, mass, fraction).

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem.symbol
documentation: Chemical symbol of element or electron.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem.count
documentation: Number of elements or electrons in composition.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem.mass
documentation: Relative mass.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.CompositionItem.fraction
documentation: Mass fraction.
Item of elemental composition.

classes.name: molmass.molmass.Composition

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Composition.total
documentation:
Sums of counts (excluding electrons), masses, and fractions.

```pycon
>>> Formula('2HO').composition().total
CompositionItem(symbol='Total', count=2, mass=18.0135..., fraction=1.0)
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Composition.dataframe
documentation:
Return composition as pandas DataFrame.

```pycon
>>> Formula('2HO').composition().dataframe()
         Count  Relative mass  Fraction
Element...
2H           1       2.014102  0.111811
O            1      15.999405  0.888189
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Composition.asdict
documentation:
Return dict\[symbol, tuple\[symbol, count, mass, fraction\]\].

```pycon
>>> Formula('2HO').composition().asdict()
{'2H': (1, 2.0141..., 0.1118...), 'O': (1, 15.9994..., 0.8881...)}
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Composition.astuple
documentation:
Return tuple\[tuple\[symbol, count, mass, fraction\], ...\].

```pycon
>>> Formula('2HO').composition().astuple()
(('2H', 1, 2.0141..., 0.1118...), ('O', 1, 15.9994..., 0.8881...))
```

Elemental composition.

The basic interface is an ordered `dict[symbol, CompositionItem]]`.

Parameters:
: items:

  : Symbol, count, mass, and fraction for all elements in formula.
    Symbols must not be repeated.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> c = Composition((('2H', 2, 4.028, 0.201), ('O', 1, 15.999, 0.799)))
  >>> c
  <Composition([('2H', 2, 4.028, 0.201), ...])>
  >>> print(c)
  Element  Count  Relative mass  Fraction %
  2H           2       4.028000     20.1000
  O            1      15.999000     79.9000
  >>> c['O']
  CompositionItem(symbol='O', count=1, mass=15.999, fraction=0.799)
  ```

classes.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry

methods.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.astuple
documentation: Return attributes (massnumber, mass, fraction, intensity, mz).

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.massnumber
documentation: Mass number, bin of spectrum.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.mass
documentation: Relative mass.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.fraction
documentation: Mass fraction or abundance.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.intensity
documentation: Fraction normalized by maximum fraction in %.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.SpectrumEntry.mz
documentation: Mass-to-charge ratio.
Entry of mass distribution spectrum.

classes.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.peak
documentation:
Most abundant entry.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').spectrum().peak.mass
194.0803...
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.mean
documentation:
Mean of all masses.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').spectrum().mean
194.1909...
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.range
documentation:
Smallest and largest massnumbers.

```pycon
>>> Formula('C8H10N4O2').spectrum().range
(194, 205)
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.dataframe
documentation:
Return composition as pandas DataFrame.

```pycon
>>> Formula('SO4_2-').spectrum(min_intensity=0.1).dataframe()
             Relative mass  Fraction  Intensity %        m/z
Mass number...
96               95.952827  0.940701   100.000000  47.976413
97               96.952996  0.008861     0.941927  48.476498
98               97.949936  0.049833     5.297443  48.974968
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.asdict
documentation:
Return dict\[massnumber, tuple\[mass, fraction, intensity, mz\]\].

```pycon
>>> Formula('DH').spectrum().asdict()
{3: (3.0..., 0.9..., 100.0, 3...), 4: (4.0..., 0.0..., 0.0..., 4...)}
```

methods.name: molmass.molmass.Spectrum.astuple
documentation:
Return tuple\[tuple\[massnumber, mass, fraction, intensity, mz\], ...\].

```pycon
>>> Formula('DH').spectrum().astuple()
((3, 3.0..., 0.9..., 100.0, 3...), (4, 4.0..., 0.0..., 0.0..., 4...))
```

Mass distribution.

The basic interface is a sorted `dict[massnumber, SpectrumEntry]`.

Parameters:

: spectrum: Mapping of mass number to relative mass and fraction.
  charge: Charge number.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> s = Spectrum({1: [1.078, 0.9999], 2: [2.014, 0.0001]})
  >>> s
  <Spectrum({1: [1, 1.078, 0.9999, 100.0, 1.078], ...})>
  >>> print(s)
  A  Relative mass  Fraction %  Intensity %
  1      1.0780000   99.990000   100.000000
  2      2.0140000    0.010000     0.010001
  >>> s[2]
  SpectrumEntry(massnumber=2, mass=2.014, fraction=0.0001, ..., mz=2.014)
  ```

classes.name: molmass.molmass.FormulaError

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.FormulaError.message
documentation: Error message.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.FormulaError.formula
documentation: Formula in which error occurred.

class_variables.name: molmass.molmass.FormulaError.position
documentation: Position in formula where error occurred.

Error in chemical formula.

Parameters:
    message: Error message.
    formula: Chemical formula in which error occurred.
    position: Position in formula where error occurred.

Examples:
    >>> Formula('abc').formula
    Traceback (most recent call last):
     ...
    molmass.molmass.FormulaError: unexpected character 'a'
    abc
    ^

    >>> Formula('(H2O)2-H2O').formula
    Traceback (most recent call last):
     ...
    molmass.molmass.FormulaError: subtraction not allowed
    (H2O)2-H2O
    ......^

    >>> Formula('[11C]').formula
    Traceback (most recent call last):
     ...
    molmass.molmass.FormulaError: unknown isotope '11C'
    [11C]
    .^
